summary: Multivariate learning increased well-predicted annotated metabolites from 333 to 366 in the IBD (PRISM) dataset.
The Spearman correlation coefficients of annotated metabolites increased from 0.259 to 0.309 when using all metabolites to train MiMeNet, with significance p < 10^-47.
MiMeNet benefits from multivariate learning by predicting the abundance of all metabolites at once, which facilitates prediction through shared information across different metabolites.
MelonnPan models each metabolite individually, missing shared information across metabolomic features that could boost prediction performance.
MiMeNet performs better than models trained on only the annotated metabolite set when noise is added to the training data.
